data.title: CBOT corn may retest support at $4.21
data.description:
CBOT March corn (CH26) may retest support at $4.21 per bushel, a break below which could trigger a fall into a wide range of $4.17 to $4.19-3/4.

The support triggered a bounce, which is considered a pullback toward a rising trendline. The uptrend from $4.17 consists of three waves.

The bottom of the wave b at $4.19-3/4 serves as target. Resistance is at $4.23-3/4, a break above which may lead to a gain into $4.24-1/2 to $4.25-3/4.

On the daily chart, the contract failed to bounce above a resistance at $4.28. The bounce might have been driven by a wave d, the fourth wave of a five-wave cycle from $4.53.

This wave is expected to be followed by the fifth wave, the wave e, which may travel into $4.10 to $4.16-1/2 range.

** Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. The views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Trump's Arctic pantomime chills the dollar
data.description:
TRUMP'S ARCTIC PANTOMIME CHILLS THE DOLLAR

The Bank of Japan did just enough to keep the yen from falling in the Asia session, nudging up inflation forecasts and doing little to dissuade the market from expecting a couple of rate hikes this year, with the first as soon as April.

The dollar was also under a bit of pressure more broadly as perhaps investors anticipate the week's Greenland drama leaves the U.S. diminished as an investment destination.

In sum, the world has a net long U.S. position to the tune of $27.6 trillion, leaving a fair bit of room for trimming.

After rescinding tariff threats, President Donald Trump said he had secured total and permanent U.S. access to Greenland in a deal with NATO, whose head said allies would have to step up their commitment to Arctic security.

European Union leaders heaved a sigh of relief and have said they want an EU-U.S. trade deal to be back on track, but warned they were ready to act if Trump was to threaten them again.

Closer to home, Trump withdrew his invitation for Canada to join his Board of Peace after Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney decried powerful nations using economic integration as weapons and tariffs as leverage in a blockbuster speech at Davos.

Equities were up in Asia, but an after-hours dive in Intel shares pointed to brewing strains in the chip supply chain. The company said it had struggled to meet demand for its data-centre server chips and its stock dropped 13%.

Meanwhile, China lifted its official yuan guidance to the stronger side of 7-per-dollar for the first time since 2023 on Friday. Although that's weaker than where market-derived expectations put the yuan's value, it's seen as a tacit approval.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

- European and U.S. PMIs

- U.S. consumer sentiment

data.title: How to Buy Gold for Investment: Gold Buying Guide for 2026 (New Release)
data.description:
Rising geopolitical tensions and global economic distress has caused a surge in the popularity of gold and other precious metals. IRAEmpire has released a new guide to help consumers make better-informed decisions.

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA / ACCESS Newswire / January 23, 2026 / Gold has long been viewed as a reliable store of value, particularly during periods of economic uncertainty. As inflation concerns, currency fluctuations, and market volatility continue to influence financial markets, many investors are exploring gold as a way to diversify their portfolios and reduce overall risk.

Overview of the Best Gold Investment Companies 2026:

  • Augusta Precious Metals (Best Overall for Large Investments)

  • Lear Capital (Best Customer Support)

  • Goldencrest Metals (Best Prices)

  • Preserve Gold (Best Products)

Michael Hunt, Senior Writer at IRAEmpire highlights, "Unlike traditional assets such as stocks or bonds, gold is a tangible investment that is not directly tied to the performance of any single economy or company. This unique characteristic has helped gold maintain its appeal across generations, from long-term wealth preservation to retirement planning."

>>Find The Best Gold Investment Company of Your State Here.

How to Buy Gold For Investment: Understanding the Motivations

According to Michael, "Investors have turned to gold for centuries as a way to preserve wealth and manage risk. One of the primary reasons gold remains attractive is its historical role as a store of value. Unlike paper currencies, which can lose purchasing power over time due to inflation, gold has tended to retain its value across long economic cycles."

Another key reason investors buy gold is diversification. Gold often behaves differently from traditional financial assets such as stocks and bonds. During periods of market stress or economic instability, gold prices have historically shown resilience, making it a useful tool for balancing portfolio risk.

>Check Out the Best Gold Companies in the US

Gold is also widely viewed as a hedge against inflation and currency depreciation. When the cost of living rises or confidence in fiat currencies weakens, investors often increase their exposure to precious metals. This demand can help support gold's value when other assets are under pressure.

Together, these factors explain why gold continues to play a role in both conservative and growth-oriented investment strategies, particularly for investors seeking long-term stability.

Different Ways to Buy Gold for Investment

There are several ways to invest in gold, each suited to different financial goals, risk tolerances, and investment horizons. Understanding these options is an important first step before deciding how to allocate capital to gold.

One common approach is buying physical gold, such as coins, bars, or bullion. This method appeals to investors who prefer tangible assets and direct ownership. Physical gold can be stored privately or in secure facilities, though it requires consideration of storage and insurance.

Another option is investing in gold-backed financial products, such as exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and mutual funds. These products track the price of gold and offer exposure without the need for physical storage. They are often favored by investors seeking liquidity and ease of trading.

Some investors gain indirect exposure through gold mining stocks. While these can benefit from rising gold prices, they also carry company-specific and market risks that differ from owning gold itself.

Finally, gold can be purchased through retirement-focused vehicles such as Gold IRAs. These accounts allow eligible gold products to be held within a tax-advantaged retirement structure, subject to specific IRS rules.

Each method has its own advantages and trade-offs, making it important for investors to choose an approach that aligns with their overall financial strategy.

Gold IRA: The Best Way to Buy Gold for Investment

For investors seeking long-term exposure to gold, a Gold IRA is often considered one of the most structured and efficient ways to buy gold for investment. Unlike purchasing physical gold outright, a Gold IRA allows eligible gold products to be held within a tax-advantaged retirement account, combining the stability of precious metals with the benefits of retirement planning.

Get this Free Gold IRA Guide on IRAEmpire

One of the primary advantages of a Gold IRA is diversification. Traditional retirement accounts are typically concentrated in stocks, bonds, and mutual funds, which can be vulnerable to inflation and market volatility. By adding physical gold to a retirement portfolio, investors gain exposure to an asset that has historically maintained value during periods of economic uncertainty.

Gold IRAs are governed by specific IRS rules designed to protect investors. Only approved gold products that meet purity standards are permitted, and the metals must be stored in secure, IRS-approved depositories. A qualified custodian manages the account, ensuring compliance, proper recordkeeping, and secure storage. While this structure introduces additional fees, it also adds a layer of oversight and protection.

Another benefit of a Gold IRA is its long-term focus. Because these accounts are designed for retirement, they encourage disciplined investing rather than short-term speculation. Taxes on gains are typically deferred, or structured according to the type of IRA, allowing investments to grow over time.

For investors who want to buy gold as part of a broader retirement strategy, a Gold IRA offers a regulated, transparent, and tax-efficient approach that aligns gold ownership with long-term financial goals.

Find the Best Gold IRA Company of Your State

Buying Physical Gold - What You Need to Know

Buying physical gold is one of the most straightforward ways to invest in the metal, as it involves direct ownership of a tangible asset. Physical gold typically comes in the form of coins, bars, or bullion, each with varying sizes, weights, and levels of purity.

Purity is an important consideration when purchasing physical gold. Investment-grade gold is usually at least 99.5% pure, though some government-issued coins meet slightly different standards while still being widely accepted. Reputable dealers provide documentation or certification verifying authenticity and purity.

Check Out the Best Gold Dealer in the US Here

Storage is another key factor. Physical gold must be stored securely to protect against theft or damage. Some investors choose home safes, while others prefer professional vault storage offered by banks or third-party facilities. Insurance may also be necessary, depending on the storage method.

In addition to storage, investors should be aware of premiums. Physical gold is typically sold at a price above the market spot price to account for minting, distribution, and dealer margins. Understanding these costs helps investors make more informed purchasing decisions.

Gold Coins vs Gold Bars: Which Is Better for Investors?

When buying physical gold, investors often choose between gold coins and gold bars. While both provide direct exposure to gold's value, they differ in liquidity, pricing, and practical considerations.

Gold coins are typically issued by government mints and are widely recognized, which can make them easier to resell. Because of their standardized weight and purity, coins often appeal to investors who prioritize flexibility and liquidity. However, coins usually carry higher premiums over the spot price due to minting and distribution costs.

Gold bars, on the other hand, are often favored by investors making larger purchases. Bars generally have lower premiums per ounce, making them a more cost-efficient option for those focused on maximizing gold content. The trade-off is that larger bars may be less convenient to sell in smaller portions.

Storage is another factor to consider. Coins may be easier to store and divide, while bars can be more space-efficient for higher-value holdings. Ultimately, the choice between coins and bars depends on an investor's budget, liquidity needs, and long-term investment strategy.

Learn About the Best Gold Dealer in the US Here

Understanding Gold Prices and Market Timing

The price of gold is influenced by a combination of global economic and market factors. Supply and demand dynamics, inflation expectations, interest rates, currency movements, and geopolitical events all play a role in determining gold prices at any given time.

Investors often encounter two prices when buying gold: the spot price and the retail price. The spot price reflects the current market value of gold traded on global exchanges, while the retail price includes premiums charged by dealers to cover minting, distribution, and operational costs. Understanding this distinction helps investors evaluate whether pricing is reasonable.

Many new investors wonder whether there is a "best" time to buy gold. While short-term price movements can be difficult to predict, gold is generally viewed as a long-term investment rather than a vehicle for frequent trading. As a result, some investors focus less on timing the market and more on gradually building exposure over time.

By understanding how gold prices work and avoiding attempts to predict short-term fluctuations, investors can approach gold purchases with a clearer, more disciplined strategy.

How to Buy Gold Safely and Avoid Common Mistakes

Buying gold for investment requires careful attention to credibility, pricing, and product selection. One of the most important steps is choosing a reputable dealer with a transparent pricing structure and a verifiable track record. Established dealers typically provide clear documentation, published pricing, and customer support throughout the purchase process.

Verifying authenticity is essential when purchasing physical gold. Investors should look for products that meet recognized purity standards and are accompanied by proper certification or assay information. Avoiding unverified sellers and unusually discounted offers can help reduce the risk of counterfeit or misrepresented products.

Another common mistake is purchasing collectible or numismatic coins when the primary goal is investment. While these items may carry historical or aesthetic value, they often come with higher premiums that are not directly tied to gold's market price. For most investors, bullion-grade gold is a more straightforward option.

Finally, investors should consider long-term storage and liquidity before making a purchase. Understanding how and where gold will be stored, how it can be sold, and what costs are involved can help prevent complications later and support a more secure investment experience.

Check Out the Best Gold Investment Companies in the USA

Buying Gold Online vs In-Person

Investors today have the option to buy gold either through online dealers or in person at local bullion shops and authorized dealers. Each approach offers distinct advantages and considerations, depending on convenience, pricing, and personal preference.

Buying gold online often provides access to a wider selection of products and competitive pricing. Reputable online dealers typically display real-time pricing, offer insured shipping, and provide detailed product descriptions. This option appeals to investors who value convenience and the ability to compare prices across multiple sellers.

Purchasing gold in person allows investors to see the product before buying and establish a direct relationship with a dealer. Local transactions can provide immediate possession and may offer additional peace of mind for those who prefer face-to-face interactions. However, local availability and pricing can vary by region.

In both cases, investors should verify the dealer's reputation, understand delivery or pickup procedures, and confirm insurance coverage during transit. Choosing between online and in-person purchases ultimately depends on comfort level, access, and investment priorities.

How Much Gold Should You Buy?

Michael shares, "Determining how much gold to buy depends on an investor's overall financial situation, risk tolerance, and long-term objectives. Gold is typically used as a diversification tool rather than a primary growth asset, which means allocation size is an important consideration."

Many investors choose to allocate a modest percentage of their portfolio to gold as a way to balance exposure to stocks, bonds, and cash. The appropriate amount can vary based on factors such as age, income stability, investment horizon, and comfort with market fluctuations. Investors closer to retirement may prioritize preservation, while those earlier in their careers may focus more on growth-oriented assets.

It's also important to consider liquidity and cash flow needs. Gold should generally be viewed as a long-term holding rather than a short-term trade. Overcommitting capital to any single asset, including gold, can reduce flexibility and increase concentration risk.

By evaluating gold as part of a broader portfolio strategy rather than in isolation, investors can make allocation decisions that support both stability and long-term financial goals.

Tax Considerations When Buying and Selling Gold

Tax treatment is an important factor to understand before investing in gold. The way gold is taxed can vary depending on how it is purchased, held, and eventually sold. Being aware of these rules helps investors avoid surprises and plan more effectively.

When physical gold is sold at a profit, it is generally subject to capital gains tax. The rate and structure may differ from those applied to traditional securities, depending on local tax laws and holding periods. Gold-backed financial products, such as ETFs or mutual funds, may also follow different tax rules than physical bullion.

Check Out this Gold IRA Tax Rules Guide.

Gold held within a retirement account, such as a Gold IRA, is treated differently. In these cases, taxes are typically deferred or structured according to the type of retirement account used, provided IRS rules are followed. However, early withdrawals or non-compliant storage arrangements can trigger penalties.

Because tax rules can be complex and may change over time, investors often benefit from consulting a qualified tax or financial professional before buying or selling gold, especially when making larger or long-term allocations.

Is Gold the Right Investment for You?

Michael shares that gold can play a valuable role in an investment portfolio, particularly for those seeking diversification and long-term stability. Its historical reputation as a store of value and its tendency to perform differently from traditional financial assets have made it a popular choice during periods of economic uncertainty.

That said, gold is not a one-size-fits-all solution. The decision to invest in gold should be based on individual financial goals, risk tolerance, and time horizon. Understanding the different ways to buy gold, the costs involved, and how it fits within a broader investment strategy is essential before making a commitment.

By approaching gold as a complementary asset rather than a speculative bet, investors can make more informed decisions that align with their long-term objectives. Careful research, disciplined allocation, and a clear understanding of the risks and benefits can help determine whether gold is the right addition to a diversified portfolio.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire is an independent financial education and comparison platform focused on retirement planning, alternative investments, and wealth protection strategies. The site provides in-depth guides, reviews, and research on topics such as Gold IRAs, precious metals investing, retirement accounts, and emerging financial tools. IRAEmpire's goal is to help readers make informed decisions by breaking down complex financial concepts into clear, practical insights. By emphasizing transparency, data-driven analysis, and educational value, IRAEmpire serves investors who want to better understand their options before choosing retirement products or investment providers.

CONTACT:

ryan@iraempire.com

data.title: Ericsson Lifts Dividend and Proposes $1.7 Billion Buyback
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

STOCKHOLM--Ericsson raised its dividend and said it would propose a buyback program of around $1.7 billion as it seeks to return cash to shareholders following the recent sale of its U.S.-based Iconectiv business and continued cost-cutting measures.

"We generated strong free cash flow and have a strong cash position today," Chief Executive Borje Ekholm said.

The Swedish telecommunications-equipment company said Friday that its key networks business posted organic sales growth of 4% in the fourth quarter as lower sales in the Americas and northeast Asia were offset by growth elsewhere.

The unit registered strong sales growth in the Middle East and Africa, as well as Vietnam. But sales declined in Latin America, where it has previously flagged intense competition and lower customer network investments, and in northeast Asia, where sales were hit by timing of network investments.

It reported an adjusted gross margin in the networks unit of 49.6%, in line with its guided 49% to 51% range, and said the margin is expected to land within a 49% to 51% range in the first quarter.

Ericsson said it would propose a share buyback program of 15 billion Swedish kronor ($1.67 billion) and declared a 2025 dividend of 3 kronor, up from 2.85 kronor in 2024.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Sanofi Says Its Amlitelimab Drug Showed Promising Results in Treating Eczema
data.description:
By Billy Gray

Sanofi said its amlitelimab drug confirmed its potential to treat eczema in two late-phase studies.

The French drugmaker said Friday that amlitelimab delivered results that supported its potential to treat patients 12 years and older with moderate to severe eczema, a condition that causes itchy, dry and inflamed skin. The treatment was well-tolerated and demonstrated a safety profile in line with previous data, it said.

Key endpoints of both studies were measured at 24 weeks of treatment in patients who had been taking the drug either every four or 12 weeks, Sanofi said. Based on the data, the company plans to proceed with global regulatory submissions for amlitelimab, it said.

Write to Billy Gray at william.gray@wsj.com

data.title: Senior US defence official Colby to visit South Korea and Japan next week, Yonhap says
data.description:
U.S. Under Secretary of Defense for policy Elbridge Colby will visit South Korea from Sunday to Tuesday and discuss military alliance issues before travelling on to Japan, South Korea's Yonhap News Agency reported on Friday.

His talks with South Korean officials will likely touch on Seoul's proposed increase in defence spending and the modernisation of the two countries' military alliance, Yonhap said, citing an unnamed source.

South Korea's Defence Ministry declined to confirm the report, saying questions should be directed to the U.S. side.

Colby, the Pentagon's top defence and foreign policymaker, leads the implementation of U.S. defence strategy.

The two countries have been discussing President Donald Trump's push for U.S. allies to shoulder a greater share of their defence cost and Washington's realignment of its military footprint to ready its troops stationed around the world for wider missions.

South Korea hosts about 28,500 U.S. troops in combined defence against North Korea's military threat. Seoul raised its defence budget by 7.5% for this year.

South Korea is pursuing the construction if its own nuclear-powered submarines, a plan Trump has backed.

data.title: Vietnam Communist Party confirms To Lam as leader for next 5 years
data.description:
Vietnam's top leader To Lam was confirmed on Friday as head of the ruling Communist Party for the next five years, according to the title under his name at a press conference which will conclude a party congress.

The press conference is to be held by the newly-appointed general secretary, an official said.

No official announcement has yet been made after a meeting of the 180 members of the central committee, elected on Thursday, who were expected to appoint on Friday the general secretary, the country's most powerful job, which incumbent Lam has bid to retain.

Lam has been party chief since mid-2024 when he replaced the late general secretary Nguyen Phu Trong.

data.title: EquipmentShare.com Prices IPO Of 30.50 Mln Class A Shares At $24.50/shr
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - EquipmentShare.com Inc, a provider of connected jobsite technology and one of the largest equipment rental companies in the United States, announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 30.50 million shares of its Class A common stock at $24.50 per share. The selling stockholders have also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4.575 million shares of Class A common stock to cover over-allotments.

The shares of Class A common stock are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 23, 2026, under the ticker symbol 'EQPT.' The Offering is expected to close on January 26, 2026.

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Wells Fargo Securities, UBS Investment Bank, Citigroup and Guggenheim Securities are acting as lead book-running managers for the Offering.
